# iOS App Baseline

Use for app-focused iOS work.

- Swift and SwiftUI are the preferred modern lane when repo facts support them.
- UIKit or Objective-C stay valid for existing legacy boundaries and migration constraints.
- SwiftData or Core Data handles structured local persistence depending on architecture and OS support.
- App Intents, widgets, extensions, StoreKit, notifications, and background work require source-grounded capability and entitlement facts.
- Swift Testing, XCTest, XCUITest, previews, accessibility checks, and TestFlight are core validation paths.

Required checks: target devices, minimum OS, Xcode project/workspace, targets, schemes, bundle IDs, package dependencies, usage descriptions, entitlements, privacy labels, third-party SDK behavior, accessibility gates, App Review constraints, signing/provisioning, versioning, and rollback.
